The latest update is out from Precision Drilling ( (TSE:PD) ).

Precision Drilling Corporation will release its first‑quarter 2026 financial results after markets close on April 29, 2026, and will host a conference call and webcast the following day to discuss the performance with investors and analysts. By offering both live participation via pre‑registration and a year‑long replay on its website, the company aims to maximize accessibility and transparency for stakeholders tracking its operational momentum and strategic progress in the energy services sector.

The company has invested heavily in digital automation through its Alpha technology platform and supports lower‑emission operations with its EverGreen environmental solutions, complemented by well service rigs, camps, rental equipment, and technical support.

data.title: Founding Director Steps Down from Arcus Development Board
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Arcus Development ( (TSE:ADG) ).

Arcus Development Group Inc. announced the resignation of founding board member Grant Longhurst as a director, effective March 30, 2026, marking the end of his roughly two decades of service to the company. The board expressed its gratitude for Longhurst’s longstanding contributions and extended well wishes for his future business endeavors, signaling a notable change in Arcus’s long-term governance lineup but without indicating any immediate operational or strategic shifts.

The departure of a founding director may prompt investors and industry observers to watch for further board changes or evolving governance practices at Arcus, though no replacement or restructuring plans were disclosed. For stakeholders, the move underscores a generational transition at the board level while the company maintains continuity under President and CEO Ian J. Talbot’s leadership.

More about Arcus Development

Arcus Development Group Inc., listed on the TSX Venture Exchange under the symbol ADG, operates in the resource development sector from its base in Vancouver, British Columbia. The company focuses on mineral exploration and development projects, positioning itself within Canada’s broader mining and exploration industry.

data.title: Hi-View Expands Toodoggone Footprint With Chip Showing Acquisition
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hi-View Resources Inc ( (TSE:GXLD) ) has shared an update.

Hi-View Resources has acquired three mineral claims totaling 157.8 hectares contiguous to its Lawyers East porphyry target in the Toodoggone District of central British Columbia, along with all associated exploration data. The Chip Showing claims sit in a geological setting favourable for copper-gold porphyry and epithermal-style gold mineralization, supported by recent rock sampling that confirmed copper, silver and gold values and indicates a robust copper-silver system with gold potential.

Management says the acquisition is part of a deliberate strategy to expand and consolidate its land position in the Toodoggone, with the new data expected to help refine targets at Lawyers East and extend the porphyry footprint to the north. The deal, which involves a modest cash payment and share issuance, strengthens Hi-View’s status as one of the largest landholders in the district and gives it greater flexibility to evaluate multiple targets and advance its exploration projects in a coordinated way.

Spark’s Take on GXLD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GXLD is a Neutral.

Hi-View Resources Inc. faces significant financial challenges with no revenue and negative earnings. While the balance sheet is strong, persistent cash flow issues pose risks. Technical analysis indicates weak momentum, and valuation metrics are unattractive. However, strategic corporate events provide a glimmer of hope for future operational improvements, which could enhance the company’s outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on GXLD stock, click here.

More about Hi-View Resources Inc

Hi-View Resources Inc. is a publicly listed mineral exploration company focused on advancing gold, silver and copper assets in the Toodoggone region of northern British Columbia. Its 100% owned and optioned projects span more than 27,791 hectares, anchored by the Golden Stranger, Lawyers and Borealis projects, complemented by the Nub and Saunders properties and additional claims under option or application.

Average Trading Volume: 114,026

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$8.3M

data.title: Rongta Technology Delays 2025 Results, Faces Trading Suspension in Hong Kong
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9881) ) has provided an update.

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co., Ltd., a PRC-incorporated joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, announced delays to its 2025 annual results due to additional audit work required by its external auditor. The company has chosen not to release unaudited management accounts, citing the risk that incomplete figures could mislead shareholders and investors.

As a result of the audit delay, the board meeting originally scheduled for 31 March 2026 to approve the 2025 results has been postponed until further notice. Trading in the company’s H shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 1 April 2026 pending publication of the audited annual results, prompting a cautionary warning to investors regarding dealings in its securities.

More about Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong via H shares. The group operates through various subsidiaries, but the announcement does not specify its particular industry segment, main product lines, or target markets.

Average Trading Volume: 86,362

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

data.title: Zhuguang Pledges Silver Grant Stake to Secure US$210 Million Senior Notes
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Zhuguang Holdings Group Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1176) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Zhuguang Holdings Group Company Limited has disclosed new security arrangements linked to its outstanding US$210 million 36‑month senior notes, involving a controlling shareholder, Rong De, and its shareholding in Silver Grant International Holdings Group Limited. The move reflects the group’s continued reliance on capital‑markets funding backed by equity stakes in other listed companies.

Rong De has entered into a share charge and a custodian deed over 679,890,022 Silver Grant shares in favour of The Bank of New York Mellon, Hong Kong Branch as security agent, with ABCI Securities acting as safekeeping agent. Any breach of these agreements would constitute an event of default under the senior notes, underscoring heightened obligations on the controlling shareholder and the importance of these securities to the group’s debt structure and noteholders’ protection.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1176) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhuguang Holdings Group Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:1176 Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhuguang Holdings Group Co., Ltd.

Zhuguang Holdings Group Company Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The group operates through various subsidiaries and has controlling shareholders that actively use capital market instruments, including U.S. dollar senior notes, to support its financing structure and strategic investments in listed entities.

data.title: Macau Legend Swings to Deeper Annual Loss on Heavy Impairment Charges
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Macau Legend Development ( (HK:1680) ) has provided an update.

Macau Legend Development Limited reported a sharp deterioration in its 2025 results, with revenue from continuing operations slipping to HK$358.6 million from HK$378.3 million and a significantly wider net loss driven by heavy impairment charges. The group booked HK$1.13 billion in impairment losses on non-financial assets, pushing its loss from continuing operations to HK$1.51 billion and total loss for the year to HK$1.57 billion, compared with a HK$622.6 million loss a year earlier.

Additional operating and administrative expenses rose while finance costs, though lower than in 2024, remained substantial, underscoring ongoing balance-sheet and profitability pressures. The swing in discontinued operations from a profit of HK$171.7 million to a loss of HK$56.4 million further weakened overall performance, resulting in a larger basic loss per share and signaling mounting challenges for shareholders and creditors as the company navigates asset quality and operational headwinds.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1680) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.25 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Macau Legend Development stock, see the HK:1680 Stock Forecast page.

More about Macau Legend Development

Macau Legend Development Limited is a Macau-focused leisure and entertainment group that develops and operates integrated resorts, gaming-related facilities and hospitality services. Listed in Hong Kong, the company targets the tourism and gaming markets in Macau and other regional locations, with revenues primarily from gaming, hotel operations and related services.

data.title: MOG Digitech Narrows 2025 Loss and Replaces Final Results After Clerical Error
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

MOG Holdings Limited ( (HK:1942) ) has issued an update.

MOG Digitech Holdings Limited reported revenue of RMB839.4 million for 2025, down from RMB1.25 billion a year earlier, but its gross profit edged up to RMB165.8 million, highlighting margin resilience despite lower sales. The group remains active in managing associates, intangible assets and property, which are subject to impairments that can materially affect its bottom line.

The company issued a clarification that its original 2025 final results announcement contained clerical errors in certain figures and has now been replaced in full by a revised version. For 2025, MOG Digitech posted a net loss of RMB90.4 million, an improvement from the RMB142.5 million loss in 2024, as the absence of large prior-year impairments and tighter control of selling expenses helped narrow losses despite higher administrative costs and new asset write-downs.

The revised results underscore ongoing financial pressure from impairments on associates and property, as well as provisions for receivables, which continue to weigh on profitability. Nonetheless, the reduced loss year-on-year may provide some reassurance to investors that the company is working through legacy asset issues even as revenue volatility and cost discipline remain key concerns for stakeholders.

More about MOG Holdings Limited

MOG Digitech Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the digital technology and related services sector. The group reports consolidated financial results in renminbi and is led by a board comprising four executive directors and three independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure.

The company focuses on technology-driven businesses that generate revenue from products and services recorded under its consolidated statement of profit or loss, with operations significant enough to involve associates and various classes of intangible and tangible assets. Its financial performance is influenced by cost of sales, administrative and selling expenses, as well as impairment charges on investments and assets, indicating exposure to both operational and investment-related risks.

Average Trading Volume: 4,193,525

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$267.6M

data.title: Main Street Capital Expands 2029 Notes via New Offering
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Main Street Capital ( (MAIN) ) has shared an update.

On March 27, 2026, Main Street Capital Corporation entered into an underwriting agreement with RBC Capital Markets to issue an additional $200 million of 6.95% notes due 2029, which were sold in a public offering that closed on March 31, 2026. These new notes form a single, fungible series with the $350 million of 6.95% notes issued in January 2024, bringing the total outstanding principal of this 2029 debt to $550 million and maintaining equal ranking with Main Street’s other unsecured obligations while remaining structurally subordinated to subsidiary-level debt.

The notes pay 6.95% interest semiannually, mature on March 1, 2029, and include standard redemption and change-of-control repurchase features, as well as covenants tied to Investment Company Act asset coverage and ongoing financial disclosure. Main Street received approximately $202.8 million in net proceeds and plans to initially use the capital to repay outstanding borrowings under its credit facilities, which should reduce near-term funding costs and give the lender additional balance sheet flexibility for future investments.

The most recent analyst rating on (MAIN) stock is a Buy with a $66.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Main Street Capital stock, see the MAIN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MAIN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MAIN is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by mixed fundamentals—strong reported profitability and balance sheet strength but persistently weak/volatile cash flow and a 2025 revenue drop. Earnings call tone and guidance/dividend actions are constructive and support the outlook, while the technical picture remains bearish. Valuation is a clear positive due to the low P/E and high dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on MAIN stock, click here.

More about Main Street Capital

Main Street Capital Corporation is a business development company focused on providing long-term debt and equity capital to lower middle market and middle market companies in the U.S. It finances management buyouts, recapitalizations, growth investments, refinancings and acquisitions, giving it diversified exposure to private and smaller public businesses across multiple industries.

data.title: International Seaways Updates Revolving Credit Facility Collateral Structure
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

International Seaways ( (INSW) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 27, 2026, an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of International Seaways entered into a joinder agreement to its existing $500 million revolving credit facility, becoming a subsidiary guarantor under that financing. As part of the agreement, the subsidiary pledged a VLCC tanker it owns as collateral, designating the vessel as a substitution asset replacing ships previously sold or released from the secured collateral pool, thereby maintaining the strength and continuity of the company’s borrowing base.

The move reflects International Seaways’ ongoing active management of its secured credit structure as it refreshes collateral in line with fleet changes. By substituting a modern VLCC into the facility’s collateral package, the company helps preserve access to liquidity under the revolving credit facility and supports financial flexibility for its tanker operations and capital allocation plans.

The most recent analyst rating on (INSW) stock is a Hold with a $71.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on International Seaways stock, see the INSW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on INSW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, INSW is a Neutral.

The score is anchored by strong balance-sheet positioning and a very positive earnings-call outlook on cash generation and shareholder returns. These positives are tempered by a concerning TTM deterioration in revenue/margins/free cash flow and by technically overbought conditions that elevate near-term pullback risk.

To see Spark’s full report on INSW stock, click here.

More about International Seaways

International Seaways, Inc. operates in the maritime transportation industry, focusing on crude oil and petroleum product shipping through a fleet that includes very large crude carriers, or VLCC tankers. The company accesses bank financing facilities to support its fleet operations and asset management strategy in the global tanker market.
